card_description: Robert Ornstein is a historical figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 3 Exegesis passages. Context: "Draft of a memorial piece for Tony Boucher."

linked_segments.summary: Draft of a memorial piece for Tony Boucher. Dick explores Boucher's fear of the universe, his role as a mentor, and the startling claim that his cat Pinky was Boucher's reincarnation.
linked_segments.title: U_Tony_01.txt

linked_segments.summary: A personal letter to Phyllis Boucher accompanying an essay about Tony Boucher. Dick discusses his current life in Fullerton, his family, and his religious development.
linked_segments.title: 1975-03-02_Phyllis_01.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick proposes to Le Guin that they are "twin outcomes" of a single underlying mystical experience. He outlines an eight-point cosmological schema involving "orthogonal time" (ontological time) versus
linked_segments.title: 1975-03-05_Ursula_01.txt
